item.title: Patton Oswalt on his new comedy special
item.description: Patton Oswalt sits down with "CBS Saturday Morning" to discuss his new special, "Tea & Scotch," and reflect on his comedic journey.

item.title: Missing Auburn University student in Japan found dead, mother says
item.description: James Higginbotham was found dead in a mountainous area outside Kyoto by a volunteer search-and-rescue group, his mother said.

item.title: Simon Pegg and Lizzy McAlpine on their film "Only What We Carry"
item.description: Simon Pegg and Lizzy McAlpine sit down with "CBS Saturday Morning" to discuss their new film, "Only What We Carry," which was shot in six days and mostly improvised.

item.title: Russian teen claims first Grand Slam tennis title with French Open win
item.description: The eighth-ranked Andreeva ended the run of 114th-ranked Polish qualifier Maja Chwalinska in the French Open final on Saturday.

item.title: U.S. doctor with Ebola recovers from deadly virus, German hospital says
item.description: Dr. Peter Stafford was working with a missionary group in the Congo when he came down with the virus last month.

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Ashley McBryde performs "Ten To Midnight"
item.description: Grammy-winning country music star Ashley McBryde's latest album, "Wild," draws on her experiences growing up in the Ozarks and her journey to sobriety. Ahead of her summer U.S. tour, here's Ashley McBryde performing "Ten To Midnight."

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Ashley McBryde performs "Bottle Tells Me So"
item.description: Grammy-winning country music star Ashley McBryde's latest album, "Wild," draws on her experiences growing up in the Ozarks and her journey to sobriety. Ahead of her summer U.S. tour, here's Ashley McBryde performing "Bottle Tells Me So."

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Ashley McBryde performs "What If We Don't"
item.description: Grammy-winning country music star Ashley McBryde's latest album, "Wild," draws on her experiences growing up in the Ozarks and her journey to sobriety. Ahead of her summer U.S. tour, here's Ashley McBryde performing "What If We Don't."

item.title: Pope Leo arrives in Spain for first papal visit in 15 years
item.description: Spaniards find themselves increasingly divided over issues including immigration, feminism and political corruption.

item.title: Condé Nast Traveler's summer travel guide
item.description: Erin Florio, the global features director at Condé Nast Traveler, joins "CBS Saturday Morning" to break down some of the best summer travel spots.

item.title: How social media exploits girlhood anxieties
item.description: Freya India joins "CBS Saturday Morning" to discuss her book "GIRLS®: Generation Z and the Commodification of Everything." India is a contributing writer to The Free Press. Paramount Skydance owns The Free Press and CBS News.

item.title: Mom of American missing in Japan says they argued over ChatGPT
item.description: James "Weston" Higginbotham went missing one week ago while on a family vacation in Japan.

item.title: How Gen Z unplugs from the digital world
item.description: Gen Z are the first to live their entire lives with the internet easily available. "CBS Saturday Morning" visits an event aimed at helping teens unplug.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Parents of missing American in Japan launch their own search
item.description: The parents of a missing 20-year-old Auburn University student have launched their own search, after their son, James "Weston" Higginbotham, went off on his own during a family trip in Japan.

item.title: The Uplift: Ellie the elephant
item.description: Meet Big Ellie, the elephant mascot of the WNBA's New York Liberty. Plus more heartwarming news.

item.title: Pope Leo could meet with Bad Bunny on Spain trip, church officials say
item.description: Pope Leo XIV began a weeklong trip to Spain on Saturday. Church officials said the pontiff could meet with Bad Bunny, who is also in Madrid as part of his world tour.

item.title: U.S. and Iran trade another round of fire
item.description: It's unclear how close the U.S. and Iran are to striking a deal, as an Iranian official says "the ball is in Trump's court," and Israel-Hezbollah hostilities continue.

item.title: Platner returns to campaign trail amid "unsettling" allegations
item.description: Graham Platner returned to the campaign trail in Maine on Friday amid more allegations about "unsettling" and "toxic" behavior toward women.

item.title: Trump touts U.S. economy to Wisconsin farmers
item.description: The U.S. added 172,000 jobs in May, according to the monthly jobs report. Meanwhile, President Trump touted his economic policies to farmers at an event in Wisconsin.

item.title: Meet the Belmont Stakes horses running in the 2026 race
item.description: The Belmont Stakes will host a New York rematch of the top two finishing horses from the Kentucky Derby today​ to wrap up horse racing's Triple Crown for 2026.

item.title: From the archives: "Ragtime" debuts on Broadway
item.description: Based on E.L. Doctorow's kaleidoscopic novel, the musical "Ragtime" tells a sweeping story of a changing America at the turn of the 20th century. In this Jan. 18, 1998 "Sunday Morning" report from the day of the show's New York opening (which also marked the debut of the Ford Center for the Performing Arts), Charles Osgood talked with Doctorow, playwright Terrence McNally, composer Stephen Flaherty and lyricist Lynn Ahrens about expanding the story and characters through music. [The show went on to win four Tony Awards.]

item.title: Prediction markets have become a draw for young men. Here's why
item.description: Prediction markets have become a draw for young men in search of quick cash and thrills, experts say. "I had almost $4,600 at one point but squandered that," one man said.

item.title: Veteran runs to fight "life-changing disease" after minor symptoms
item.description: Dr. Sara Whittingham thought she would know if something was wrong. But her minor symptoms had a surprising cause.

item.title: Spearfisher killed in Australia's third fatal shark attack in 4 weeks
item.description: The 35-year-old man was spearfishing with family when he was attacked by a shark on Saturday, police said.

item.title: Israeli forces kill Palestinian baby in the West Bank: Health ministry
item.description: Sam Fahd Abu Haikal was killed Friday evening, and his parents were wounded, the Palestinian health ministry said.

item.title: Wemby misses at the end, Knicks beat Spurs for 2-0 lead in NBA Finals
item.description: The red-hot Knicks are going home, two wins away from an NBA championship that the capital of the world has been waiting to see for generations.

item.title: Employers added 172,000 jobs in May, surging past expectations
item.description: The labor market continues to show strength despite rising inflation and concerns about slowing economic growth.

item.title: Ned Jarrett, NASCAR Hall of Famer and CBS announcer, dies at 93
item.description: Ned Jarrett was inducted into the NASCAR Hall of Fame in 2011 after 50 career wins on the sport's top circuit.

item.title: Tribeca at 25: NYC festival celebrates music, Broadway and New York stories
item.description: The 25th edition of the film and media festival opens on Wednesday, showcasing more than 100 world premiere documentaries and narrative features, as well as short films, live music, podcasts and conversations with leading entertainment figures.
